title: The infant with a megaloblastic anemia that ignores B12 and folate
blurb: A pyrimidine-synthesis defect: megaloblastic anemia plus orotic crystals in the urine — but here the ammonia is normal, unlike the urea-cycle mimic.

patient.complaint: Failure to thrive, pallor, and gritty orange urine crystals.

opening: 7-month-old boy with poor growth, pallor, and developmental delay. CBC shows a macrocytic anemia with hypersegmented neutrophils; it did NOT respond to B12 or folate. Urine has orange crystals; urinary orotic acid is very high — but plasma ammonia is NORMAL. What do you want to ask, examine, or order next?
history: Failure to thrive from a few months of age, recurrent infections (leukopenia). No vomiting or encephalopathy. Trials of B12 and folate failed. Parents consanguineous (autosomal recessive). No hyperammonemic crises.
exam: Pale, small for age, mild hypotonia and developmental delay. No hepatosplenomegaly. No jaundice. Cardiopulmonary normal.

prompts.mechanism: Briefly: why a megaloblastic anemia unresponsive to B12/folate, and how do you tell this from OTC deficiency (both raise orotic acid)?

pinpoint.prompt: Click the pyrimidine-synthesis step that fails in hereditary orotic aciduria.
pinpoint.correctMsg: Yes — UMP synthase. Orotate can't be converted to UMP, so pyrimidine nucleotides run short (impairing DNA synthesis → megaloblastic anemia, leukopenia, failure to thrive) while orotic acid spills into the urine. Because the block is in synthesis (not the urea cycle), ammonia is normal — and treatment is oral URIDINE, which bypasses the block.
pinpoint.wrongMsg: Not quite. The block converts orotate into the first pyrimidine nucleotide (UMP).
pinpoint.revealMsg: Target: UMP synthase (Orotate → UMP). Oral uridine replenishes pyrimidines downstream of the block and suppresses orotate overproduction.

mcqs.stem: Why doesn't the megaloblastic anemia respond to B12 or folate?
choices: The defect is in pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis itself, so cells lack the UMP-derived nucleotides needed for DNA regardless of folate/B12 status
The patient is also iron deficient
B12 and folate are not absorbed
mcqs.explanation: Folate/B12 deficiencies cause megaloblastic anemia by impairing thymidylate/purine synthesis. Here the problem is a primary inability to make UMP (pyrimidines), upstream of any folate role — so supplementing folate/B12 does nothing. Uridine is the fix.

mcqs.stem: Both hereditary orotic aciduria and OTC deficiency raise urinary orotic acid. What distinguishes them?
choices: Hereditary orotic aciduria has NORMAL ammonia + megaloblastic anemia; OTC deficiency has HYPERAMMONEMIA without anemia
mcqs.explanation: In OTC deficiency, carbamoyl phosphate overflows into pyrimidine synthesis → orotic acid PLUS hyperammonemia (a urea-cycle defect). In hereditary orotic aciduria the urea cycle is intact (normal ammonia), but pyrimidine synthesis fails → megaloblastic anemia. Ammonia is the discriminator.

choices: Oral uridine (or uridine triacetate), which supplies pyrimidines downstream of the block and feedback-suppresses orotate overproduction
mcqs.explanation: Uridine bypasses UMP synthase, restoring the pyrimidine pool (correcting the anemia and growth) and reducing orotate excretion via feedback. It is a rare 'treatable' inborn error with a clean dietary-style fix.
